admission_summary: Admitted for an evidence-only inventory of existing compact representations and deterministic evaluation of hypothetical distance-aware graph-neighborhood packing. The admitted slice preserves selection_effect none, does not mutate ContextPackManifest.items, introduces no persisted compact representation contract, and makes no context-selection, provider, prompt, CLI, automatic inclusion, graph persistence, retrieval, embedding, reranking, index, memory-store, or runtime-manifest changes. GAP-0002 remains open.
constraints: Implementation is authorized only within the scope admitted by WARR-20260716-0006.
Any scope expansion beyond WARR-20260716-0006 requires a separate proposed and admitted plan.
Any admitted implementation must remain evidence-only with selection_effect none.
Do not change graph_neighborhood_context_items or build_latest_context_manifest selection behavior.
Do not change scripts/build_context_pack.py, ask_provider.py, compare_providers.py, provider prompts, or prompt rendering.
Do not change CLI behavior or introduce new CLI flags in this slice.
Do not infer graph targets automatically or enable automatic graph-local context inclusion.
Do not persist compact representations or graph state.
Do not add retrieval, embeddings, reranking, production index mutation, memory-store mutation, or runtime-manifest mutation.
Do not classify hypothetical representation as implemented capability.
expected_outputs: A deterministic inventory of compact representation coverage across the audited artifact corpus.
A read-only distance-aware packing evaluation module with selection_effect none.
A structured evidence report comparing whole-artifact and hypothetical distance-aware scenarios.
Separate diagnostics for lineage isolation and token-budget exclusion.
A decision-ready recommendation to reuse existing representations, propose a new compact contract, or reject the distance-aware approach.
Capability, verification, warrant, tests, and self-model records if the admitted evaluation is implemented successfully.
mitigation: Inventory representation coverage first within the admitted evidence slice, label all compact scenarios hypothetical, use deterministic token accounting, report isolated targets separately, and require a new governed plan before adding persisted representation contracts or context-selection behavior.
next_action: Implement only the evidence-only inventory and evaluator admitted by WARR-20260716-0006. Keep GAP-0002 open and require new governance before any persisted compact contract or context-selection behavior.
non_goals: Adding or editing authoritative lineage relations.
Persisting a graph database or graph index.
objective: Determine whether existing repository representations are sufficient to evaluate and later support compact graph-local context within realistic token budgets, or whether a separate compact-representation contract must first be proposed.
proposed_change: After a separate admission checkpoint, add a deterministic, read-only evidence evaluator that inventories existing compact representation coverage and performs hypothetical distance-aware token-budget analysis. The evaluator will report evidence only and will not modify context manifests or selection behavior.
rationale: The repository-wide audit found authoritative and bounded graph neighborhoods: 33 of 43 targets had neighborhoods, all selected paths were authoritative, and the maximum neighborhood contained six candidates.
The same audit found whole-artifact representation pressure: neighborhood costs ranged from 494 to 13,208 tokens, with median cost 2,223 tokens.
At 500-token budgets many targets retained no neighbor, and at 1,500 tokens many retained only one neighbor.
COMP-0027 records independent provider agreement that representation granularity is the demonstrated blocker, while target-selection semantics and persisted compact contracts are premature.
COMP-0027 also supports inventorying existing L0, L1, ABS, and related representations before deciding that a new compact representation contract is required.
The ten lineage-isolated targets represent a separate diagnostic class and should not block evaluation across the 33 connected targets.
risk: The principal risk is treating existing summaries as suitable graph-local representations without measuring coverage or semantic fitness. A second risk is treating hypothetical compact scenarios as implemented behavior. A third risk is conflating lineage isolation with budget starvation.
scope: Inventory existing L0 chunk summaries, L1 episode and artifact summaries, ABS abstractions, and other structured compact representations relevant to the 43-artifact audit corpus.
Measure representation availability, schema provenance, and deterministic token-cost distributions.
Evaluate all 33 connected targets under the existing whole-artifact baseline and hypothetical rich distance-one plus compact distance-two scenarios.
Evaluate representative graph budgets including 500, 1,000, 1,500, 2,000, and 3,000 tokens.
Report neighborhood coverage, retained-neighbor counts, distance-one and distance-two token shares, and reasons for exclusion.
Classify the ten lineage-isolated targets separately from token-budget-excluded targets.
Assess whether existing compact representations are sufficient for later implementation or whether a new persisted compact-representation contract should be proposed separately.
Keep graph-local context distinct from global ontology, invariants, and governance context.
Keep GAP-0002 open unless a separate future closure verification and warrant support closure.
success_criteria: The admitted plan validates through the self-model schema, references WARR-20260716-0006, and is indexed as admitted and open.
The admitted plan cites GAP-0002, CAP-0005, CAP-0009, CAP-0011, VERIFY-20260716-0004, WARR-20260716-0005, WARR-20260716-0006, and COMP-0027.
Any later evaluator inventories representation coverage before drawing conclusions about compact representation fitness.
Any later evaluator preserves selection_effect none and does not mutate ContextPackManifest.items.
Evaluation distinguishes lineage-isolated targets from token-budget-excluded candidates.
Evaluation reports deterministic token and coverage metrics for all 33 connected targets.
Evaluation treats hypothetical compact representations as diagnostic scenarios, not implemented behavior.
No new persisted compact representation contract is introduced in this slice.

No provider, prompt, context-selection, CLI, automatic target-selection, automatic-inclusion, graph-persistence, retrieval, embedding, reranking, index, memory-store, or runtime-manifest behavior changes.
Targeted tests, full repository tests, and self-model audit pass before any implementation capability is registered.
summary: Admit an evidence-only evaluation of existing compact artifact representations and hypothetical distance-aware graph-neighborhood packing. The slice inventories available L0, L1, ABS, and other compact representations; compares the whole-artifact baseline against rich distance-one and compact distance-two scenarios; and diagnoses lineage-isolated targets separately. It preserves selection_effect none and introduces no context-selection, provider, prompt, CLI, persistence, retrieval, embedding, reranking, index, memory-store, or runtime-manifest changes.
title: Evaluate compact representation coverage and distance-aware graph-neighborhood packing
